name: Zed (agent)
description: Agentic editing inside the Zed editor

developer.comment: Zed Industries, Inc. — creators of Atom and Tree-sitter.

first-release.comment: The agentic Agent Panel launched May 7, 2025. Zed's earlier AI assistant panel (chat + inline transformations, not agentic) shipped Aug 20, 2024 (https://zed.dev/blog/zed-ai). Using the agentic Agent Panel date since this candidate is the agent.

status.comment: Actively developed; Zed reached 1.0 in 2026. Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Zed editor (including the agent) is GPL-3.0-or-later with Apache-2.0 components where marked; the ACP protocol and Zeta model are also open source.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent for code generation, refactoring, debugging.

interface.comment: Agent Panel lives inside the standalone Zed editor (a desktop app). No separate CLI or web form for the agent.

host-ides.comment: Runs inside Zed, a standalone editor; not an extension hosted in another IDE.

open-source.comment: The agent's source is part of the open-source Zed editor (GPL-3.0-or-later).

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: The agent is a built-in feature of the standalone Zed editor.

default-model-provider.comment: Hosted models include Claude (Anthropic) and GPT (OpenAI); supports Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama and 8+ providers. Claude is the most prominently featured default.

model-choice.comment: User can select among many models/providers within the Agent Panel.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK supported for Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral and others; unlimited use with your own API keys on the free plan.

local-model-support.comment: Supports local models via Ollama and OpenAI-compatible servers.

multi-file-editing.comment: Agent plans and applies coordinated edits across many files (demoed editing 50 files), reviewable in a multibuffer diff.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agent discovers context, edits files, and runs commands, staying on task until done with tool-calling loop.

terminal-command-execution.comment: The Write profile includes a tool to run terminal commands (with user confirmation by default).

mcp-support.comment: Extends agent tools via MCP servers (documented, shipped).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Threads sidebar runs multiple agent threads at once, each independent with its own context and conversation, plus worktree isolation. Treated as parallel agents; no explicit subagent-spawning primitive.

codebase-indexing.comment: Agent searches the codebase ad hoc with no prior setup or indexing; context is retrieved on demand (@-mentions, search), not a prebuilt semantic embeddings index.

planning-mode.comment: No explicit dedicated plan-then-execute or read-only planning mode is documented. The Ask profile is read-only (no edits) but is not framed as a plan-first phase; could not confirm a distinct planning mode. Checked 2026-06-02.

multimodal-input.comment: Image input supported for capable models (OpenAI GPT-4o and later, Anthropic Claude 3 and later, etc.).

browser-computer-use.comment: No built-in browser/GUI-driving capability for the agent to navigate and verify changes; not documented.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Zed ships a model-callable spawn_agent tool: 'Spawns a subagent with its own context window to perform a delegated task. Useful for running parallel investigations, completing self-contained tasks, or performing research where only the outcome matters.' The agent itself decides to delegate mid-task (each subagent inherits the parent's tool access), so this is model-driven fan-out, not just the user-launched parallel threads in the Threads Sidebar. Added ~v0.227 (subagent depth configurable). Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Edit Prediction (inline-completion), conversational Agent Panel (interactive-chat), and tool-calling agent that defaults to per-action confirmation (supervised-agent). Can be set to auto-run for a more autonomous posture, but runs in-editor (not detached async).

inline-completion.comment: Edit Prediction provides as-you-type tab completions, powered by Zed's open-source Zeta model (alternatives: Copilot, Mercury Coder, Codestral).

background-async-execution.comment: Agent runs within the open editor session; no detached cloud/background dispatch where the user can close the editor and walk away. Multiple threads run in parallel but require the editor open.

persistent-memory.comment: Project context via .rules/AGENTS.md and skills is always-on project instruction, not learned cross-session memory. No documented automatic memory carried across distinct sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Each agent edit exposes a 'Restore Checkpoint' button to revert the working tree to before that change.

git-integration.comment: Native Git panel with staging, diffs, and commits; the agent can generate commit messages and changes are reviewed in a multibuffer diff.

pr-creation.comment: No native built-in PR creation/management in the editor (active community requests). PR creation is possible via an MCP server tool but is not a first-party feature.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports .rules plus .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, .clinerules, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENT(S).md, CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md auto-included as project-level instructions (first match in priority order).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, custom agent profiles, and reusable Skills (slash commands); Zed also has an extension API.

permission-model.comment: tool_permissions default is 'confirm' (prompt per action); configurable to 'allow' (auto-run) or 'deny'. Profiles (Write/Ask/Minimal) further gate tool access.

self-host-on-prem.comment: No documented on-prem/self-hosted deployment of the agent service. Users can point at local models (Ollama) or their own API keys, but the editor/agent isn't deployed on the user's own server infrastructure.

privacy-mode.comment: Zed states it doesn't store prompts or code context; sharing data for model improvement is strictly opt-in (one-time, per instance). Business plan can lock data-sharing off org-wide.

enterprise-controls.comment: Zed for Business ($30/seat) offers org-wide AI model policies, data-governance enforcement, and spend controls, but SSO/SAML, SCIM, audit logs, RBAC, VPC and SOC 2 are roadmap, not yet shipped (checked 2026-06-02). None of the defined tags are currently met.

pricing-model.comment: Free Personal plan; Pro $10/mo subscription with $5 token credits then usage-based at API list price +10%; unlimited free use with your own keys (BYOK). Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: Personal plan is free forever (2,000 edit predictions + unlimited use with your own API keys).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pro plan $10/month (unlimited edit predictions + $5 token credits). Checked 2026-06-02; was $20 at the May 2025 launch.

maturity.comment: Editor reached 1.0 (2026) and is polished/fast with a stable agent panel; agentic features still evolving rapidly, so 4 rather than 5.

community-size.comment: ~84k GitHub stars for the editor; growing but the agent itself is newer and smaller than Cursor/Copilot scale.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Has an extensions ecosystem plus MCP server support and shareable Skills.

project-memory-files.comment: Agent auto-includes the first matching file in priority order: .rules, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, .clinerules, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENT.md/AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md. 'custom' covers Zed's own .rules plus .windsurfrules/.clinerules/GEMINI.md which have no dedicated tag. Checked 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: Project-level (.rules at project root; project-local skills in <worktree>/.agents/skills/) and user/global (Rules Library defaults; global skills in ~/.agents/skills/). No enterprise-policy or per-folder/subdirectory rule scope is documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: The .rules file is always-on (auto-included in every Agent Panel interaction). Skills are agent-requested — the model sees each skill's name+description and pulls the full body in on demand via the skill tool (or the user @-mentions/slash-invokes it). No path/glob- or file-type-based conditional rule activation is documented.

auto-memory.comment: No documented mechanism for the agent to automatically write cross-session memories on its own. Memory is via manually-authored .rules and Skills files only. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-support.comment: Zed Skills (v1.4.0+) are reusable, packaged agent capabilities (SKILL.md + bundled resources) loaded on demand; they replaced the older Rules Library as the recommended way to package reusable instructions.

skill-invocation.comment: User-callable via /skill-name slash command or @-mention; model-callable by default (the agent sees the skill catalog and calls the skill tool when a task matches). Model invocation can be turned off per skill with 'disable-model-invocation: true'.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: only each skill's name + description sit in the system prompt by default; the full SKILL.md body and bundled resources load on activation. Docs advise keeping bodies under 500 lines and delegating detail to reference files.

skill-distribution.comment: Installed by git clone (e.g. from the skills.sh registry), manual folder placement into ~/.agents/skills or <worktree>/.agents/skills, or GitHub-URL import via the command palette; a built-in create-skill skill scaffolds new ones. No curated one-click in-app marketplace.

sandboxing-technology.comment: No OS sandbox, container, or VM isolation for command/tool execution. The agent runs tools with the user's full privileges; safety is enforced purely by the permission (confirm/allow/deny) regex engine, not by isolation. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: tool_permissions sets a per-tool default plus always_allow/always_deny/always_confirm Rust-regex rules matched against tool-specific inputs: terminal commands (command-prefix / per-command patterns like 'git .*'), file paths (per-path), and fetch URLs (per-domain). Profiles add a read/write distinction (Ask = read_file/grep only, no edit_file/terminal; Write enables editing+commands). Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: Fixed priority ladder: built-in security rules > always_deny > always_confirm > always_allow > tool default > global default. Deny always wins over allow; there is no user-controlled evaluation order or gitignore-style specificity re-override, so deny-overrides (not ordered or nested-exceptions). Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: User authors declarative allow/deny/confirm regex rules in settings.json (tool_permissions). No code-level hook/policy-script decision point that inspects the action and rewrites it, so declarative-rules rather than programmable. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No default deny patterns for .env or secret files. The only hardcoded built-in rules block recursive deletes (rm -rf / , ~, ., .. etc.) for the terminal tool; docs state 'there are no other built-in rules' and point users to uncomment example deny rules themselves. So secrets are not protected out of the box. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: The 'Always for...' option writes a durable always_allow (or tool-level default) rule into settings.json, which survives restarts and prevents re-prompting for the same action. ('Allow once' is session-only and would not count.) Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Setting tool_permissions.default to 'allow' bypasses confirmation prompts for most tools (a near-unrestricted, hands-off posture); always_deny, always_confirm, built-in rm -rf rules, and paths in Zed's settings dir still gate. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: No sandbox-level egress blocking; outbound network is open by default. The fetch tool's URLs can be gated by always_allow/always_deny regex patterns (per-domain), so network is configurable but otherwise unrestricted. No default blocked-egress or proxy enforcement documented. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box tool_permissions.default is 'confirm' — every permission-gated tool call (edits and commands) prompts for approval until the user configures allow rules. Checked 2026-06-12.

auto-accept-mode.comment: No quick, session-scoped 'accept edits' toggle (nothing like Claude Code's Shift+Tab). The only ways to stop per-action prompting are persistent config: setting tool_permissions.default to 'allow' (the full bypass captured by Unsupervised Mode) or using the per-tool 'Always for...' menu, which writes durable always_allow/tool-default rules to settings.json rather than a reversible in-session mode. Per the guideline, a tool whose only non-prompting option is the full bypass is false here. Checked 2026-07-18.
